content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901            322-6441           322-6444

September 20, 1979


Mary K. Thompson
Darwin Community Services
District
P.O. Box 97
Darwin, CA 93522

Dear Ms. Thompson:

     Thank you for your letter dated August 2, 1979 seeking advice
regarding the disqualification provision of the Political Reform
Act. The facts, as I understand them, are as follows:

     Mr. Bill Wells, one of the Directors of the Darwin Community
Services District (District) owns the only gas station in the city
of Darwin. The question you have asked us to consider is whether
it is permissible under the Political Reform Act for the District
to purchase gasoline from Mr. Wells' station.

     The Political Reform Act does not prohibit the District from
purchasing gasoline from Mr. Wells' station but it does require
Mr. Wells to refrain from making or participating in the making
of the District's decision. Government Code Section 87100 of the
Act provides,

     No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason
to know he has a financial interest.

     Section 87103 provides,

     An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably

Ms. Mary K. Thompson
September 20, 1979
Page Two


     foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally, on:

     (a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

     (c) Any source of income, other than loans by
a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or pro-
mised to the public official within twelve months
prior to the time when the decision is made; . . .

     In this case, the gas station represents both an investment
and a source of income to Mr. Wells. Obviously, it is foreseeable
that Mr. Wells' station will be affected by the District's decision
to purchase gasoline. Therefore the only remaining question is
whether the effects of the District's decision will materially
affect Mr. Wells' financial interests.

     Commission Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(A)
provides that an official's source of income is materially affected
when the amount of the official's income will be increased or
decreased by the decision in question. Therefore, since the
District's decision will affect the amount of income to be received
by the gas station, and hence by Mr. Wells, he must refrain
from participating in the District's decision.

     Although the Political Reform Act does not preclude the
District from contracting with boardmembers, Government Code
Section 1090 may. Since the Commission has no jurisdiction over
Section 1090, I recommend that you consult with your District
Attorney's Office. (See also Attorney General Opinion requested
by the District Attorney of Alpine County, 57 Ops. Cal. Atty.
>458 (Volume 57, Page 458.)

     I hope that my advice is helpful. If you have any questions
please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        _________________
                                        Dwight Dickerson
                                        Legal Counsel
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:nrw

DARWIN COMMUNITY SERVICES DISTRICT
Post Office Box 97
Darwin, California 93522

August 2, 1979

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

I have a question regarding Conflict of Interest and a
Deputy District Attorney of Inyo County referred me to
you.

The Darwin Community Services District was formed for the
purpose of supplying water to the small town of Darwin,
California.

An old discarded fire truck has recently been donated to
the District. With volunteer labor this truck is now in
running condition and the District will be using it to
carry tools and materials to work on our antiquated
water line. In the past people of the town have been using
their own vehicles for this purpose.

In Darwin we have only one gas station and we are 40 miles
from any other station. Bill Wells who recently purchased
this gas station is a Director on the Board of the Darwin
Community Services District. The question is this: Can
the District purchase gas from Bill Wells' gas station
for this truck without any conflict of interest being
involved?

Would appreciate your answer.

Thank you.

Mary Kay Thompson
Secretary

sections.question: The Darwin Community Services District was formed for the purpose of supplying water to the small town of Darwin, California. An old discarded fire truck has recently been donated to the District. With volunteer labor this truck is now in running condition and the District will be using it to carry tools and materials to work on our antiquated water line. In the past people of the town have been using their own vehicles for this purpose. In Darwin we have only one gas station and we are 40 miles from any other station. Bill Wells who recently purchased this gas station is a Director on the Board of the Darwin Community Services District. The question is this: Can the District purchase gas from Bill Wells' gas station for this truck without any conflict of interest being involved?
sections.conclusion: Although the Political Reform Act does not preclude the District from contracting with board members, Government Code Section 1090 may. Since the Commission has no jurisdiction over Section 1090, I recommend that you consult with your District Attorney's Office.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Darwin Community Services District can purchase gasoline from a gas station owned by one of its Directors, Bill Wells, without violating conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit the District from purchasing gasoline from Mr. Wells' station, but Mr. Wells must refrain from making or participating in the District's purchasing decision because the purchase will have a material financial effect on his gas station income. The Commission recommends consulting the District Attorney regarding potential restrictions under Government Code Section 1090.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Letter dated September 20, 1979 from Legal Counsel Dwight Dickerson responding to August 2, 1979 request from District Secretary Mary Kay Thompson. Contains both incoming request and FPPC response. Clear statutory citations and regulatory analysis provided. Minor confidence deduction due to incomplete citation quote (Section 87103 text cuts off mid-sentence).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Darwin Community Services District was formed for the purpose of supplying water to the small town of Darwin, California. An old discarded fire truck has recently been donated to the District. With volunteer labor this truck is now in running condition and the District will be using it to carry tools and materials to work on our antiquated water line. In the past people of the town have been using their own vehicles for this purpose. In Darwin we have only one gas station and we are 40 miles from any other station. Bill Wells who recently purchased this gas station is a Director on the Board of the Darwin Community Services District. The question is this: Can the District purchase gas from Bill Wells' gas station for this truck without any conflict of interest being involved?

CONCLUSION: Although the Political Reform Act does not preclude the District from contracting with board members, Government Code Section 1090 may. Since the Commission has no jurisdiction over Section 1090, I recommend that you consult with your District Attorney's Office.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that while the Political Reform Act permits the Darwin Community Services District to purchase gasoline from a gas station owned by Director Bill Wells, Mr. Wells must abstain from participating in the purchasing decision because it will materially affect his income. The Commission recommends consulting the District Attorney regarding potential additional restrictions under Government Code Section 1090.
